patch.value:
## Context
classifyItem in the separate pm-changelog repo (src/generator.ts) checks Removed via hasAny(allValues, [removed,remove,deleted,delete]) which runs before the strong-tier Changed needles and the bug-like-type default. allValues includes the sanitized title; the sanitizer strips --flags but not plain hyphenated words.

## Evidence
- pm-changelog src/generator.ts (Removed rule ordering + hasAny word-boundary match)
- Live repro: closing pm-z5nb ('...remove-type, add-status...') produced a '### Removed' changelog line for a plan that removed nothing (reverted in this PR by reopening pm-z5nb)

## Acceptance criteria
- See structured acceptance_criteria field.
pm-changelog classifier mis-routes feature titles containing the word 'remove'/'delete' into the Removed section

The pm-changelog classifyItem 'Removed' rule fires on \bremove\b/\bdelete\b anywhere in allValues (strong tier + sanitized title) BEFORE the strong-tier Changed/type defaults. A closed item whose title legitimately contains 'remove-type' as part of a feature name (e.g. the schema-customization plan 'Complete schema customization epic: remove-type, add-status, ...') is published under '### Removed', which misleads readers into thinking a capability was removed. The existing flag-stripping guard only neutralizes --flags, not plain words like 'remove-type'.

- A title containing 'remove'/'delete' only as a substring of a feature/command name is NOT routed to Removed when type+tags carry no removal signal
- Strong-tier (type/tags) signals can override the weak title-only Removed match (mirroring the Changed/Added precedence)
- Regression test covers 'remove-type'/'create_default_type'-style titles
- Verified against the live pm-cli tracker (e.g. pm-z5nb no longer mis-routes)
